fact: October 19 is exactly 292 days into the year, which means there are only 73 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, squirrels are frantically burying nuts right now - they can bury up to 10,000 acorns in a single season and forget where half of them are!
The sun is setting almost 2 minutes earlier each day around October 19 in most places, making bedtime feel like it comes super fast.
October 19 falls smack in the middle of Libra season, which means if you're born today, your zodiac symbol is a scale - the only zodiac sign that's not a living thing!
Maple trees are dumping their leaves like crazy right now in cold places, and a single big maple can drop over 600,000 leaves in one autumn!
Why do spiders seem to be everywhere in mid-October? Because this is mating season for house spiders, so they're running around looking for partners!
The air pressure changes around this time of year make some people's joints ache, but it also makes soap bubbles last longer because the thicker air supports them better.
October 19 is roughly 28 days after the autumn equinox, which means we're losing daylight at the fastest rate of the entire year in northern places.
Woolly bear caterpillars are crawling everywhere right now searching for the perfect spot to hibernate - and some people think their stripes predict how harsh winter will be!
In Australia and other southern places, October 19 means spring is in full swing and magpies are swooping people because they're protecting their babies!
The moon looks extra orange when it rises around this date because you're seeing it through more of Earth's atmosphere, which filters out blue light and leaves the warm colors.
Geese flying south right now can travel over 1,500 miles without stopping, and they honk constantly to keep the flock together - like a really loud, flying road trip!
